path: Problem: Conditionally aggregate values by two criteria, but fall back to aggregating by one criterion when the two-criteria combination does not exist
Action: SUMIFS with dynamic criteria driven by IF + existence test using MATCH over an INDEX-produced array
Observation: Test whether any row satisfies (key1=target1)*(key2=target2); if none, switch the second SUMIFS criterion to a non-restrictive condition (e.g., "<>") so only the first criterion filters
